Phase 9 — ANN, Voting & Stacking Ensembles Run 1

Generated 2026-04-12 08:46  |  Train: 2021–2024  |  Test: 2025  |  Dataset: Exp3-S2 (ADD + CONSIDER features)
Phase 9 — Advanced Models: Three new model classes evaluated on Experiment 3 Sub-2 feature sets (broadest scope, 20–32 features per target):
• ANN — sklearn MLPRegressor tuned via GridSearchCV (5 architectures × 4 alpha values = 20 combos, TimeSeriesSplit n_splits=3).
• Voting Ensemble — VotingRegressor combining Ridge + ElasticNet + RF with equal weights. Base models use tuned hyperparameters.
• Stacking Ensemble — StackingRegressor: base models RF + Ridge + ElNet → Ridge meta-learner trained on OOF predictions (KFold n_splits=5). Final evaluation on the 2025 holdout set.

Compared against best prior models: ElNet, Ridge, RF (all from Exp3-S2, run 1). Highlighted cell = best Test R² in each row across all 6 models.

Phase 9 model
Win counts: ElNet: 4W | Ridge: 0W | RF: 0W | ANN: 1W | Voting: 1W | Stacking: 2W. ElNet leads overall.
Average Test R²: ElNet +0.351 | Ridge +0.331 | RF +0.260 | ANN -1.123 | Voting +0.353 | Stacking +0.084.
ANN performance: Average Test R² = -1.123 — MLPRegressor overfits badly on composite targets (R² gap up to +6.8). Likely cause: temporal distribution shift to 2025 combined with insufficient training data (~470 samples) for MLP generalisation. ANN is not recommended for deployment without more data.
Voting Ensemble: Avg Test R² +0.353 vs prior best ElNet +0.351 (+0.002). Voting provides the best balance of performance and stability.
Stacking: Avg +0.084 — competitive on Grab targets but fails on Comp pH (R²=-1.564). Meta-learner (Ridge) inherits the composite pH difficulty.
Composite COD: Remains unsolved — best R² across all models = -0.024. This target likely requires external process variables not currently measured.
